steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: On the left of the blank is MoMA (noun, subject). On the right is the noun phrase “a vast collection of oil paintings.” The blank sits in a noun clause between these two structural elements.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
A noun clause requires a finite verb form as its main verb. Houses is a finite verb in the simple present tense that functions as the main verb of this clause.

B, C, and D are all non-finite forms that cannot serve as the main verb of a clause.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
